name: @chbrain/khai-engine-temperament
description: khai engine content, the temperament domain: the stable disposition a persona arrives with and returns to -- the resting baseline the room reads when nothing in particular is happening.
license: SEE LICENSE IN LICENSE and LICENSE-CODE

khai.tagline: Temperament as position: the stable disposition a persona arrives with and returns to -- five axes, two poles each, the resting baseline the room reads when nothing in particular is happening.

card.wire: Attaches at Position. The anchor (position_temperament.md) declares the resting baseline a persona arrives with and returns to -- the disposition that tilts every act without deciding any one of them, and that the room reads when nothing in particular is happening. The law makes every persona carry one.
card.issue: Ten pole positions across five axes a persona may hold -- extraverted/reserved, reactive/steady, open/grounded, driven/easygoing, warm/forthright (the Big Five). Each pole is adaptive and each carries its own cost; a persona holds a read on each axis, sits there by default, and pays a standing price to act off it. Where virtue fires under pressure, temperament sits.
card.require: Two altitudes hold the engine. The law: declared once in the project's Instructions Knowledge chapter, linking the anchor, so the baseline is read for every persona. The link: each persona carries, under Projection, its read on each axis it is given -- the pole it sits nearer.
card.enforce: Structure is checked; coherence is judged. The kit verifies the floor: the law is declared and each persona links a real temperament pole. Whether the baseline is true to the persona, and held consistently beneath the situational firing, is semantic, taught and reviewed, never failed by the suite.
card.setup: Declare the law in the Instructions Knowledge chapter, linking the anchor. Then give the persona its baseline under Projection: a pole on each axis, each carrying the anchor upward. Read it in what the persona does without trying and returns to when the pressure lifts, never as a label.
